Paper_Title: Du et al. - Improving Factuality through Multiagent Debate
Claim_Text: 3 agents x 2 rounds debate improves accuracy by +7-16pp over single agent (Arithmetic: 67->81.8, GSM8K: 77->85, MMLU: 63.9->71.1)

Paper_Title: Liang et al. - Encouraging Divergent Thinking in LLMs through Multi-Agent Debate
Claim_Text: Degeneration-of-Thought (DoT): LLM commits to answer and reflection cannot shift thinking. MAD framework uses tit-for-tat debate + judge to counter this

Paper_Title: Wang et al. - Mixture-of-Agents Enhances LLM Capabilities
Claim_Text: Collaborativeness phenomenon: LLMs produce better output when seeing other models' outputs

Paper_Title: Li et al. - More Agents Is All You Need
Claim_Text: Small model x many agents > large model x few agents (under certain conditions). More agents + sampling-and-voting -> consistent improvement

Paper_Title: La Malfa et al. - Large Language Models Miss the Multi-Agent Mark
Claim_Text: 60+ papers analyzed: most MAS LLM lack autonomy, social interaction, structured environment. Natural language coordination is ambiguous/lossy

Paper_Title: Google DeepMind - Towards a Science of Scaling Agent Systems
Claim_Text: Coordination cost T=2.72*(n+0.5)^1.724. Effective team size ~3-4 agents. Single agent accuracy >45% needed for diminishing returns
Self-MoA (same model) > MoA (different models) on AlpacaEval 2.0 by +6.6%. Quality trumps diversity

Paper_Title: Smit et al. - Should we be going MAD?
Claim_Text: MAD is highly sensitive to hyperparameters. Basic MAD often doesn't beat self-consistency

Paper_Title: Wynn et al. - Talk Isn't Always Cheap: Failure Modes in Multi-Agent Debate
Claim_Text: Debate can REDUCE accuracy even when strong model is majority. Models switch correct->incorrect prioritizing agreement over accuracy. Echo chamber effect

Paper_Title: Han & Zhang - Exploring Advanced LLM MAS Based on Blackboard Architecture
Claim_Text: Competitive with SOTA MAS on commonsense/reasoning/math benchmarks while using fewer tokens

Paper_Title: Liu et al. - Lost in the Middle: How Language Models Use Long Contexts
Claim_Text: Middle position information causes 30%+ accuracy drop in long contexts
Achieves Spearman rho 0.47 (vs baseline 0.15-0.36) via auto persona extraction + multi-agent in-group debate + aggregator
